x-artifact.note: Design-first artifact only — describes the MCP tool surface, not a running server. Tool input/output schemas $ref the same object schemas as the OpenAPI; each tool maps to an OpenAPI operationId. Surfaces BSA's open case data (applications, resolutions, the legacy calendar index, pre-application meetings), publishes the hearing calendar that has no dataset today, and adds file_variance_application — the net-new write workflow that today is a paper PDF-form intake with no online portal.

server.name: io.nyc.bsa
server.title: NYC Board of Standards & Appeals (BSA)
server.description: Agent-native access to the NYC Board of Standards & Appeals — zoning applications and their status, written resolutions, the public hearing calendar, pre-application meetings, and the property/zoning-lot index (back to 1916) from NYC Open Data and nyc.gov, plus the net-new ability to file and track a variance / special permit / extension / appeal that today is a paper PDF-form process with no online portal.
server.instructions: Use find_applications / get_application to locate BSA cases by borough, application type (BZ variance/special-permit, BZY extension, SOC amendment, Appeal), status, zoning district, council district, or BBL, and get_application_resolution or find_resolutions to reach the written decision (today a linked PDF). find_hearings publishes the public hearing calendar that has no Open Data twin. find_pre_application_meetings covers the pre-filing consultation log. find_zoning_lots looks up a property's BSA history including legacy cases back to 1916. file_variance_application (net-new) files a new case; it requires an authenticated filer and mutates state — confirm the relief sought, the ZR/GCL section, the premises, the applicant, and the required exhibits with the user before submitting. BSA assigns the calendar number.

tools.name: find_applications
tools.title: Find Applications
tools.description: Search BSA applications / cases by borough, application type, status, zoning district, council district, BBL, date range, and/or free text.

tools.name: get_application
tools.title: Get Application
tools.description: Fetch a single BSA application / case (type, section, premises, status, decision) by its calendar number.

tools.name: get_application_resolution
tools.title: Get Application Resolution
tools.description: Fetch the written resolution / decision for a BSA case by its calendar number (today a linked PDF).

tools.name: find_resolutions
tools.title: Find Resolutions
tools.description: Search BSA resolutions (decisions) by status, application type, borough, or date range.

tools.name: find_hearings
tools.title: Find Hearings
tools.description: List the BSA public hearing calendar (date, session type, virtual/in-person, webinar link) — data that today exists only as HTML/PDF on nyc.gov.

tools.name: find_pre_application_meetings
tools.title: Find Pre-Application Meetings
tools.description: List BSA pre-application meetings (applicant, date, application type, premises) — the pre-filing consultation log.

tools.name: find_zoning_lots
tools.title: Find Zoning Lots
tools.description: Look up a property / zoning lot and its BSA case history by BBL, BIN, or block/lot — including legacy cases back to 1916.

tools.name: list_my_variance_applications
tools.title: List My Variance Applications
tools.description: List the authenticated filer's own submitted BSA applications and their status — today only trackable by contacting BSA, since there is no online portal.

tools.name: file_variance_application
tools.title: File a Variance Application
tools.description: File a new BSA case — a zoning variance, special permit, extension of time, or appeal of a DOB determination. NET-NEW workflow with no online portal today — filing is a paper PDF-form process. Requires an authenticated filer. Mutates state — confirm the relief sought, the ZR/GCL section, the premises, the applicant, and the required exhibits before submitting. BSA assigns the calendar number.
